qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection via unsanitized comment and hintComment in NativeQueryBuilder
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SQL injection via unsanitized comment strings in FindOptions.comments/hintComments/indexHint fields, while the advisory describes SQL injection via duck-typed object detection that interprets specially crafted objects as raw SQL fragments. Different root causes: the advisory root cause is duck-typed detection of ORM marker properties in object assignment/update APIs (wrap().assign(), em.nativeUpdate(), em.nativeInsert(), em.create()), while the finding root cause is missing sanitization of SQL comment delimiters in comment-related APIs (QueryBuilder.comment(), FindOptions.comments). Different affected code paths: advisory affects object property interpretation in core ORM APIs, finding affects comment string embedding in query builder.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes SQL injection in MikroORM via duck-typed detection of internal ORM marker properties when specially crafted objects are passed to wrap().assign(), em.nativeUpdate(), em.nativeInsert(), or em.create() APIs. The 'sql-injection-via-unsanitized-comment' finding describes a different SQL injection vector via unsanitized comment strings in FindOptions.comments/hintComments/indexHint, which is a distinct code path with a different root cause (missing comment delimiter sanitization vs duck-typed object marker detection).
evidence: Advisory root cause: duck-typed detection of ORM marker properties in object-based APIs (lines in advisory: 'vulnerability was caused by duck-typed detection of internal ORM marker properties')
Finding root cause: missing sanitization of */ delimiter in SQL comment string interpolation (NativeQueryBuilder.ts:195, 590, 637)
Advisory affected APIs: wrap(entity).assign(userInput), em.nativeUpdate(), em.nativeInsert(), em.create() followed by em.flush()
Finding affected APIs: EntityManager.find(), QueryBuilder.comment(), QueryBuilder.hintComment(), QueryBuilder.indexHint()
Different vulnerability mechanisms: object property interpretation vs comment string embedding
